summary: Superb 1970 Winterland soundboard capture featuring fiery Pigpen performances, a scorching “Dancin’ in the Streets” and explosive drum‑jam sections.
review: The April 15 1970 Winterland show shines as a tight, energetic set that lets the band “sit round the fire” and play without pressure.  Highlights include Pigpen’s powerhouse renditions of “This Is A Man’s World” and “Turn On Your Lovelight”, the crowd‑pleasing “Dancin’ in the Streets”, and an almost legendary drum‑jam that many reviewers compare to a Santana solo.  The soundboard source is praised as crystal‑clear and “sweet”, making this recording one of the most listenable from the early ’70 era.

ai_rating.rationale: All nine substantive reviews are 4‑5 stars and uniformly praise both performance and audio quality; recording is confirmed as a high‑quality soundboard, and the show receives repeated commendations for energy, song selection, and drum jams.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Multiple reviewers call the mix crisp, sweet, and “Super 70' Soundboard”. No complaints about drop‑outs or muffiness; the transfer is praised as high fidelity.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Reviewers describe a relaxed, fire‑circle vibe where the band moves freely, delivering tight acoustic gems and relentless drum‑jam energy that keep the momentum flowing from start to finish.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Mentioned fondly in venue memories (“Jerry and Pig”) and as part of the fun, relaxed atmosphere.
band_member_comments.Keys: Pigpen’s organ/vocals are highlighted as “magic” on his two numbers, underscoring his central role this night.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drum‑jam segment is repeatedly praised as primal, insane, and comparable to Santana’s “Soul Sacrifice”, marking it a show‑defining moment.
